prompt:
Format-NodeCard in nodecard.ps1 renders the canonical host cards on our rack sheets, and it still parses $args by hand like it is 2009: count the arguments, index into the array, hope for the best. Two things finally pushed this onto my plate. First, inventory lives in CSVs now and I want `Import-Csv inventory.csv | Format-NodeCard` to just work -- one card per row, bound off the row's properties. Second, last week someone called it with a misspelled flag and instead of an error it happily minted a card named after the flag.

Modernize it into a proper advanced function:

- `[CmdletBinding()]` with a real param block: -Name (mandatory), -Site (default 'hq'), -Role (default 'app'), -Ip (optional). All four bind from the pipeline BY PROPERTY NAME, so Import-Csv rows with site/role/ip columns flow straight in; our CSVs spell the name column 'host', so -Name needs an alias for that. Extra columns like 'rack' must be ignored. Piping N rows yields N cards in row order.
- Positional compatibility is non-negotiable: `Format-NodeCard web01 lyon edge 10.4.2.7`, and every shorter prefix of that shape, must keep producing exactly today's cards. Format-RackSheet in the same file calls it positionally and must keep working WITHOUT being edited.
- Named invocations in any order work too, e.g. `Format-NodeCard -Ip 10.1.2.3 -Name DB01`.
- Validation grows teeth: -Role only accepts app|edge|db|batch; -Ip, when non-empty, must be a dotted quad (digits and dots -- nothing fancier needed); calling with no name, five positionals, or an unknown flag like -Nmae is a caught-able terminating error under -NonInteractive, never an interactive prompt and never a silently wrong card.
- Card text itself is frozen: `<name>.<site>.grid.internal [<role>]` plus ` ip=<ip>` only when an ip was given; name/site lowercased exactly as today.

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_nodecard.ps1` from the workspace root. Hands off the test file.
notes: 6P7 item 3: parameter modernization ($args positional -> [CmdletBinding()] advanced function). files/ ships a WORKING $args-parsing function plus Format-RackSheet, a positional caller that must survive unedited (proves the compat requirement structurally). Baseline: compat driver fully green; modern driver fails because piping into a $args function throws the usage error, named flags get eaten as positionals, and a typo'd -Nmae mints a card instead of erroring. PowerShell-native surface: ValueFromPipelineByPropertyName + [Alias('host')] binding from Import-Csv rows (probe-verified 2026-07-16), Position=0..3 compat pins, ValidateSet/ValidatePattern producing catchable errors under -NonInteractive (MissingMandatoryParameter probe-verified), process{} per-pipeline-item emission. Differentiation: 6P1's ps-argrelay/ps-tokenpass are BUILD seeds around CLI arg forwarding/binding of scripts; this is the refactor angle -- a working function's calling convention modernized under compat pins; no existing seed pins pipeline-by-property-name binding.
